Christiansborg Palace
One of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. Make sure to bring an adapter if your devices use a different plug type.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros, so be sure to exchange your money.
There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. Visitors can reach anywhere by metro. It's the most convenient way to get around the city.
The weather in Copenhagen in December is below -15 degrees Celsius. Make sure to pack warm clothing.
The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen. So, communication shouldn't be a problem.
The “hello” in Danish is “hej”, “thank you” in Danish is “farvel”, “goodbye” in Danish is “tak”. It's always good to know a few words in the local language.
